title: aster humistratus
intro: Aster humistratus, also known as Wood's Aster, is a perennial plant native to the western United States, characterized by its white or pink flowers and hairy stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its chemical composition of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of Aster humistratus are believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system. Aster humistratus can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use, although its safety and efficacy have not been extensively scientifically studied.
